coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of how large language models are served through APIs
Elementary knowledge of prompts and how they affect model responses
Ability to call a hosted model from a script with supervision
Basic understanding of tokens and how they relate to cost and limits
Ability to log model inputs and outputs following examples
Elementary knowledge of reading dashboards and basic metrics
Basic understanding of what an evaluation is and why it matters
Ability to use version control and notebooks for experimentation
complementaryCompetencies: Basic knowledge of Python or a scripting language
Familiarity with JSON and configuration files
Elementary understanding of API keys and rate limits
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Can complete simple tasks with detailed guidance
Needs 3-6 months to be productive

coreCompetencies: Ability to build simple LLM-powered features with minimal supervision
Understanding of prompt versioning and keeping prompts in source control
Ability to create small evaluation datasets and run them against a model
Practical knowledge of measuring latency, token usage and cost per request
Ability to add structured logging and tracing to LLM calls
Understanding of retrieval-augmented generation at a basic level
Ability to configure retries, timeouts and basic error handling
Knowledge of comparing outputs across models and prompt variants
complementaryCompetencies: Basic knowledge of vector databases and embeddings
Ability to use containers to package an LLM service
Basic understanding of continuous integration for prompts
indicators: Can build simple LLM features independently
Resolves medium complexity issues in LLM pipelines
Actively participates in code and prompt reviews

coreCompetencies: Mastery of prompt management with versioning, testing and rollout

Ability to build observability for LLM systems including quality and drift signals
Deep knowledge of retrieval pipelines including chunking and indexing strategies
Ability to optimize cost and latency through caching and model routing
Understanding of guardrails for input and output filtering
Ability to set up A/B tests and offline comparisons for prompt changes
Knowledge of monitoring token consumption and setting budgets
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of prompt injection and data leakage risks
Ability to build feedback collection loops from users
Basic understanding of fine-tuning workflows
indicators: Completes complex LLM operations tasks with minimal supervision
Identifies and resolves quality and performance regressions
Actively contributes to technical design of LLM systems

coreCompetencies: Ability to design end-to-end LLM pipelines from ingestion to serving

Ability to implement guardrails and content safety across the pipeline

indicators: Leads development of critical LLM infrastructure components
